title: Revise PROP-009 applying hostile-review findings and concrete drop-in fixes
summary: PROP-009 (tinker's response to DIRECTIVE-002 — pop-without-review precondition) was submitted and sent to a one-shot hostile review. Verdict: REJECT, with 3 critical findings, 4 major, 3 moderate, 3 minor. The kernel of truth — diagnosis correct, fix direction right — is preserved; the issues are execution quality (patch byte-exactness, env-var plumbing that doesn't actually flip, soft-fallback collapse under multi-push-single-review, wrong-way operator-escape-hatch, missing retroactive audit, FUSE/clone race). A second agent produced concrete drop-in fixes for every critical and major finding, plus all moderate and minor. This directive bundles both the hostile review and the fixes; tinker's job is to produce PROP-009r (revised) applying the fixes, with explicit pushback on any fix tinker believes is wrong. Operator will then run tinker manually (not on scheduled cron) and approve or re-reject.

context.what_happened: DIRECTIVE-002 asked tinker to diagnose the decider popping queue items without matching curmudgeon reviews. Tinker produced PROP-009 proposing: (a) strict queue_id match + soft substring+timestamp fallback, (b) 3-day shadow period with ${PROP_009_ENFORCE:-false} env-var toggle, (c) operator escape hatch via require_matching_review_file:false, (d) stale-item 7-day escalation to attention-inbox. Hostile review found the env-var toggle is broken (shell substitutes at script-expansion time before node ever sees it), the escape hatch is inverted relative to DIRECTIVE-002's guidance, the soft fallback can double-claim one review for multiple pushes, the retroactive audit DIRECTIVE-002 explicitly asked for was deferred, and the FUSE/clone race is not closed (shadow log records false-positives only, not false-negative stale reads).
context.what_is_already_correct_in_PROP_009: Diagnosis (substring match shared by decider Step E2 and curmudgeon Step 0b) — correct. Fix direction (strict queue_id + soft fallback) — correct. Committing curmudgeon to write queue_id going forward — correct. Correctly ruling out H2 (dedup auto-drain) and H4 (batch-drain) with evidence. Flagging the SEC-4.1.c3 cycle-numbering race as orthogonal — correct. Preserve all of that.
context.what_must_change: Patch format (store find/replace as raw-text arrays of lines, joined with \n, with mandatory dry_run_check grep preflights). Flag-file (monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag) instead of env var for shadow→enforce toggle. Review-consumption tracking via popped_by_queue_id written onto review files at pop-time. Narrower operator bypass requiring pushed_by contains 'operator' AND require_matching_review_file:false AND non-empty operator_bypass_reason. queue_id threaded through 6 specific locations in curmudgeon.md. Retroactive audit: 23/50 history entries are bug-pops (14-Apr:6, 15:4, 16:1, 17:3, 18:9); recommend ≤3 blocks/day threshold for enforce-flip. FUSE+clone dual-read via unionReviewFiles(). Cycle-number anomaly filed as ISS in open-issues.json rather than deferred. 7-day stale-item escalation downgraded to log-only (defer inbox write to a future PROP).

deliverable_requested.primary: Produce PROP-009r (revised) in monitor/tinker/proposals/ — may overwrite PROP-009.json in place or file as PROP-009r.json, tinker's call. Apply all concrete fixes from the bundled guidance below.
secondary: Produce a short DISAGREEMENTS.md (or embed in PROP-009r under a 'disagreements_with_review' field) listing any hostile-review finding or concrete fix tinker believes is wrong, with reasoning. Genuine dissent is welcome — the operator preference is to hear both sides. Do not silently drop fixes; reject them explicitly with a reason the operator can evaluate.
For each of the 3 critical + 4 major + 3 moderate findings, PROP-009r MUST state its resolution status: 'fixed_as_specified', 'fixed_differently' (with explanation), or 'declined' (with reasoning).
Preserve the verification_pattern, files_to_modify structure, and existing approval_required flags from PROP-009. Do NOT change the proposal metadata shape — only the contents.
Run the retroactive audit fresh in tinker's run and confirm the 23/50 number plus the per-day breakdown. If tinker's count differs, explain why.
format_constraints: Patches in files_to_modify[].find_lines and .replace_lines MUST be JSON arrays of raw-text lines (not single escaped strings). Each line stored as it appears in the source file, with the JSON encoding applied once (so a file-literal backslash becomes \\ in the JSON source, \ when decoded).

Every file_to_modify MUST carry a dry_run_check object with a grep -Fq command that passes ONLY when the find block is present verbatim in the source file. The applier runs the dry_run_check before attempting any replacement.

Do NOT use shell env-var interpolation (${VAR:-default}) for runtime toggles. Use a flag file (monitor/decisions/prop-009-enforce.flag) whose presence=enforced, absence=shadow. Document the touch/git-rm recipe in the proposal.

bundled_hostile_review_verbatim:
The history entries do NOT record `pushed_at`. But the soft-match doesn't need history—it needs the **queue item's** pushed_at, which IS set (line 272 in decider.md, set when pushed). That's fine. I have enough to write the review.

---

**REJECT**

PROP-009 correctly identifies the symmetric substring-match bug and the fix direction is right, but the proposal has several blocking defects and at least two landmines that would ship broken code.

## Critical

- **The decider find-string is not byte-exact. Patch will fail to apply.** `decider.md` line 194 contains literal comments (`// Check each queue item — if a review file exists for it, pop it`) and line 199 is `const hasReview=reviews.some(f=>searchTerms.some(t=>f.includes(t)));`. PROP-009's `find` block quotes the outer `node -e "` script, which *is* present in the file (lines 184-214), so the raw text ought to match — BUT the `find` string in the JSON has been re-escaped once (newlines as `\n`, regex as `\\d+`) in ways the `find`/`replace` protocol must un-escape exactly. The JSON stores `/^part(\\d+[a-z]?)-(.+)$/` in `find` but the file contains `/^part(\d+[a-z]?)-(.+)$/` — one backslash each. Whichever tool consumes the patch must pick one convention; PROP-009 doesn't specify which. This is an unforced footgun. **Require** the author to demonstrate the patch applying via a dry-run `grep -Fq` before approval.

- **The `${PROP_009_ENFORCE:-false}` "flip to true after 3 days" plan is broken.** Shell does `${VAR:-default}` substitution at script-expansion time, so `enforce` is resolved to a literal `false` inside the `node -e "…"` string before node ever runs. To flip it, you must either (a) set `PROP_009_ENFORCE=true` in the decider's scheduled-task shell environment (and PROP-009 admits it hasn't confirmed that plumbing exists — a Grep for `process.env` / `PROP_009_ENFORCE` in decider.md returns no other examples), or (b) edit the prompt file. The migration plan leans on (a) but never proves it works. Audit-criteria gating is theater if the flip mechanism is unverified. **Require** either: plumb the env var explicitly with a one-line setter block earlier in the run, OR collapse the shadow/enforce toggle into a hardcoded boolean the operator flips in a follow-up commit.

- **Soft-fallback collapses on the existing review corpus.** Grep shows only **71 of 303** review files carry a `queue_id` field. The ones that DON'T — and they are the majority including `WIN-058.c2.json`, `WIN-053.c2.json`, `WIN-058b-post-EXP102.c2.json` — will fall into the soft fallback (substring + `reviewed_at > pushed_at`). For the current queue (items pushed 2026-04-18T10:09Z), *any* future curmudgeon run that produces a new `WIN-058.c3.json` will carry `reviewed_at > 10:09Z` and will validly pop qid 177 — good. But also: **every unrelated re-push of WIN-058 from now until an old review naturally ages out will satisfy the soft fallback off a single post-pushed_at review written for an earlier push.** Example: decider pushes qid 200 (WIN-058) at 2026-04-25. The review written for qid 177 has `reviewed_at` ≈ 2026-04-19 > 2026-04-18 ≪ 2026-04-25 ... actually wait, that's fine because pushed_at moves forward. Real risk: **multi-push-single-review.** Decider pushes qid 179 (SEC-4.1) at T0, curmudgeon writes a review with `reviewed_at=T1` covering qid 179 (with `queue_id=179`). Decider pushes qid 181 (SEC-4.1 again) at T2. At T2 the qid-179 review's `reviewed_at=T1 < T2`, so soft-match fails — GOOD. But if curmudgeon didn't include queue_id and the review was written at T3 > T2, the *same* review satisfies the soft fallback for **both** qid 179 AND qid 181 even though curmudgeon only reviewed once. PROP-009 doesn't address this. **Require** soft-fallback to mark a review "consumed" after it pops a queue item, preventing reuse on a subsequent push.

## Major

- **Backward-compat operator escape hatch is exactly backwards.** DIRECTIVE-002 explicitly lists in escape_hatches #3: *"Operator-pushed items should ALSO have the precondition enforced — human pushes are the minority case that would most disrupt a deadlock."* PROP-009 inverts this: `pushed_by=operator` + `require_matching_review_file:false` returns to the buggy substring match. Current queue items 176/177/178 have `require_matching_review_file: true` set by the operator, so in practice the bypass is off — but the *default* is unspecified for future operator pushes, and the prompt text offers no guidance. **Require** making `require_matching_review_file: true` the enforced default and constraining the bypass to a narrowly-named `require_matching_review_file: "substring-only-legacy"` literal that must be set explicitly per push with a human-note justification.

- **curmudgeon.md patch relies on `queue_id` being written by curmudgeon, but no current review-writing instruction in curmudgeon.md tells it to include queue_id.** Grep shows the only `queue_id` mention in curmudgeon.md is in an example schema (line 72). The write-side instruction PROP-009 adds says "always include the `queue_id` field" but this is buried inside the Step 0b explanatory paragraph. The actual WIN/section/proposal/prediction-batch review procedures at Step 3 and the review-template sections do NOT thread queue_id through. **Require** adding queue_id to each concrete review schema/example downstream, plus a pre-write assertion.

- **Retroactive audit that DIRECTIVE-002 specifically asked for is not done.** Directive deliverable #2: *"produce a list of all queue history entries from 2026-04-15 onward where popped_by=decider and no curmudgeon review file with matching queue_id exists."* PROP-009 notes the history is capped at 50, waves at git archaeology ("follow-up task for tinker"), and reports only the 10+2 confirmed in the visible window. The 5-per-day threshold used to gate enforcement is therefore uncalibrated — an operator approving this proposal has no evidence base for what "normal" shadow-period blocks look like. **Require** a git-log-p walk of priority-queue.json covering 2026-04-15 → present before the audit threshold is set.

- **FUSE/clone race window not closed.** Decider Step E2 reads `reviewsDir` from `WORKSPACE` (FUSE). FUSE can serve stale content (see CLAUDE.md "FUSE Staleness Warning"). Curmudgeon writes reviews to FUSE and separately to its clone (workspace-sync). If a curmudgeon run finishes at 11:02Z and decider runs at 11:03Z, decider's `readdirSync(WORKSPACE/...)` may not yet see the new file — meaning the strict/soft match fails and an item stays on the queue. Under enforcement this is mostly safe (item will pop next run). But the *shadow-period* behavior pops anyway while logging — so we won't detect this class of race in the shadow log at all. The log records only false-positive blocks, not false-negative stale reads. **Require** reading review filelist from both CLONE and WORKSPACE and union-merging, as the prompt already does in several places.

## Moderate

- **Cycle-number anomaly deferred to an "orthogonal PROP" that isn't filed.** PROP-009 says "Recommend tinker pick up as a separate PROP if pattern repeats in next 7 days." Directive-002's deliverable #4 asked for this to be addressed or flagged explicitly as orthogonal. It's flagged but not actionable — no ticket, no watcher, no date beyond "if pattern repeats." This fades into the background. **Require** a tracking item filed in `monitor/decisions/open-issues.json` before close.

- **Stale-item 7-day escalation is dead code in practice.** Current churn pops items within hours. A 7-day threshold will rarely fire; when it does, it only `console.log`s — it does not write to `attention-inbox.json` despite the summary claiming it does. CLAUDE.md flags attention-inbox as multi-writer with concurrent-write discipline. PROP-009's summary advertises an escalation that the patch does not implement. **Require** either removing the advertised escalation or writing it correctly (pull-rebase, JSON merge, push), with multi-writer conflict handling.

- **Coordination fragility.** PROP-009 says "ship both patches in the same commit" — fine — but does not say who enforces this. The proposal is approved-as-a-unit by the operator and applied by hand; if the decider patch lands first and curmudgeon's doesn't (FUSE sync hiccup, manual slip), shadow period is benign. If curmudgeon lands first writing queue_id but decider still uses substring match, pops go through the old path anyway — also benign. Genuine risk is low, but the proposal should state "apply decider.md first, verify shadow log, then apply curmudgeon.md" for a clean rollback story.

## Minor

- The `history` schema change (new `pop_reason` field) is not documented in `reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md`. Integrity agent may flag on next run.
- `CLONE` is interpolated via `${CLEAN_CLONE}` but PROP-009 doesn't verify this env var is always set in decider's execution context (existing code assumes it, so probably fine — but worth a grep).
- `pushed_at` guess in evidence table for qid 165 says "2026-04-18T07:26Z" with "_guess" suffix — this data is retrievable from git log of the queue file; ambiguity shouldn't remain at proposal-writing time.

## Kernel of truth

The diagnosis is correct: both sides of the pop/skip protocol share a substring-match defect that silently drains post-integration re-reviews. The fix direction — strict `queue_id` match with a substring-plus-timestamp soft fallback, plus operator-escape-hatch — is the right shape. PROP-009 also correctly ruled out H2 (dedup auto-drain) and H4 (batch-drain logic) with evidence from the actual prompt text, identified the stale-state echo SEC-4.1.c3 cycle-numbering race as orthogonal (correctly), and committed curmudgeon to writing queue_id going forward — which is the durable architectural improvement regardless of any shadow/enforcement tuning. Preserve all of that. The problems above are about execution quality (patch byte-exactness, env-var plumbing, soft-fallback reuse, retroactive-audit rigor, and honoring the directive's explicit guidance on operator-push enforcement), not diagnostic accuracy.
operator_preferences: The operator prefers tinker to think about the other side of each fix before adopting it. If a hostile-review finding is wrong or a fix overreaches, say so with evidence.

The M1 operator-bypass narrowing (requires explicit operator_bypass_reason string) is NEGOTIABLE — if tinker believes silent bypasses are better for operational velocity, argue it and leave the field optional; do not unilaterally choose.

Mod2 chose 'remove 7-day escalation from summary' over 'implement it properly'. If tinker thinks the full multi-writer attention-inbox write is tractable in this PROP, propose it as an alternative with concurrency-handling code.

Mod3 ordering (curmudgeon.md first, then decider.md, then flag) is the recommended order but not load-bearing. If tinker prefers single-commit shipping of both prompt files, argue the case — the hostile-review 'coordination fragility' finding notes genuine risk is low either way.
escape_hatches: If the dry_run_check preflight reveals PROP-009's find-strings genuinely do match the source files byte-for-byte (and C1 was a false alarm about JSON escaping), note this, keep the existing strings, and add the dry_run_check as defense-in-depth rather than rewriting all patches.

If tinker's fresh retroactive audit returns a different unmatched-count, use tinker's number — the 23/50 figure was produced by one agent on one pass and may have classification errors; tinker's run is the audit of record.

If the flag-file approach is blocked by the FUSE unlink constraint on a file that lives in the clone (it isn't, but if tinker finds an interaction), escalate with reasoning rather than inventing a third mechanism.
concerns_to_address: Risk that PROP-009r, by becoming comprehensive, ships too many changes in one commit and becomes hard to roll back. MITIGATION: enforce Mod3's apply-order (curmudgeon first, verify, then decider, verify, then flag) — this already gives 3 natural rollback points.

Risk that the hostile-review agent's byte-exactness concern (C1) is a false positive — the find-strings in PROP-009.json may already decode correctly when the JSON is parsed and the strings applied raw. MITIGATION: tinker runs the dry_run_check grep commands against the actual prompt files in its clone before concluding C1 is or isn't real, and reports the result in DISAGREEMENTS if C1 turns out to be benign.

Risk that M2 (thread queue_id through 6 curmudgeon.md locations) creates a large prompt diff that drowns out the substantive change. MITIGATION: tinker may consolidate into fewer, more load-bearing edits if the intent is preserved — the 6 locations were a starting sketch, not a contract.

Risk that the operator approves PROP-009r without re-hostile-reviewing it, re-introducing execution-quality bugs. OPERATOR DECISION: steve will either (a) approve PROP-009r directly after reading tinker's output, or (b) spawn a second hostile review to check that all prior findings are addressed. Choice is made at directive-completion review time.
completion_criteria: Tinker produces PROP-009r with all 3 critical + 4 major + 3 moderate findings marked fixed or explicitly declined with reasoning. The fresh retroactive audit number is stated. DISAGREEMENTS section exists (may be empty if tinker agrees with everything). Dry_run_check commands are present on every file_to_modify entry. Operator reviews PROP-009r and either approves for application or sends back another directive.

cross_references.hostile_review_agent_id: aef325222858becc1 (one-shot, session-internal — full text bundled above)
cross_references.concrete_fixes_agent_id: a81941475f2d9a5b6 (one-shot, session-internal — full text bundled above)

execution_note_for_steve: Run tinker manually (not on the 10:30 daily cron) so the PROP-009 revision picks up these fresh findings without waiting for the next scheduled run. After tinker produces PROP-009r, review it, then either approve+apply (using the apply-order in Mod3) or send another revision directive.
